title: Bucket truck overturns into oncoming traffic; worker seriously injured
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A bucket truck overturned into oncoming traffic on Memorial Drive, Cambridge, injuring a worker seriously.
The injured worker, in the bucket at the time, was hospitalized; their identity remains undisclosed.
Memorial Drive remains closed for several hours for recovery and investigation by OSHA and State Police.

title: Healey congratulates WHOOP on plans to expand, add 600 jobs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: WHOOP plans to add 600 jobs, with most based at its Kenmore Square headquarters in Boston.
Governor Maura Healey applauded WHOOP's expansion, highlighting Massachusetts as a hub for innovation and talent.
Founded by Will Ahmed, WHOOP offers health monitoring memberships and is expanding globally with $400M in venture backing.

title: Here’s the Eagles’ reported asking price for a Patriots A.J. Brown trade
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Eagles' asking price for A.J. Brown involves a potential first-round and second-round pick deal.
A.J. Brown recorded 78 catches, 1,003 yards, and seven touchdowns during the 2025 season.
Mike Vrabel's past connection to A.J. Brown could influence Patriots' trade negotiations.

title: Here are the Mass. snow totals from Tuesday’s wintry mix
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Several Massachusetts towns reported over 6 inches of snow from Tuesday's fast-moving storm.
Freezing rain followed the snow, with Haverhill receiving 0.1 inches and Sterling 0.13 inches.
Reported snow totals included 6.5 inches in SW Northfield and 6 inches in Warwick.

title: The best movies on Netflix right now (March 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's best movies list includes 'The Lego Movie,' 'Misery,' and 'Zombieland' for March 2026.
Genres covered on the list include drama, comedy, horror, action, documentary, and family movies.
'Erin Brockovich,' 'Ex Machina,' and 'The Irishman' are highlighted as top drama picks on Netflix.

title: After losing her brother to suicide, she’s running Boston to help others feel less alone
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Jen Hoye is running the Boston Marathon in memory of her brother Teddy, who died by suicide in 2017.
Hoye, 53, from Taunton, runs to support Samaritans and their work with the 988 crisis lifeline and suicide loss survivors.
She dedicates the last mile of the marathon to Teddy, making the race deeply personal and meaningful.

title: Residents voice frustrations with snow removal during City Council hearing
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Residents expressed frustration with Boston's snow removal efforts during a City Council hearing following two major snowstorms.
Complaints focused on inadequate sidewalk clearing, impacting people with disabilities and leaving curb cuts blocked by snowbanks.
The city considered options like purchasing snow melting machines and creating a volunteer 'snow corps' to assist during storms.

title: RFK Jr. blasts Dunkin’ as Healey replies, ‘come and take it’
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: RFK Jr. criticized Dunkin' for high sugar content in drinks during an 'Eat Real Food' rally in Austin, Texas.
Governor Maura Healey responded humorously to Kennedy's remarks, sharing a Dunkin' meme saying 'come and take it.'
Kennedy's criticism prompted social media reactions with references to New England's revolutionary history and a recent Dunkin' campaign.

title: Stats in Southie is closing for now, but has plans to reopen ‘bigger and better’
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Stats Bar & Grille in South Boston is closing at the end of March but plans to reopen in 2027.
The new Stats location will be part of a five-story, mixed-use development on Dorchester Street.
Owned by Andrew and Jim Statires, the revamped bar will occupy a two-story space, pending construction outcomes.

title: Dine at the Boston table famous for JFK’s proposal to Jacqueline Bouvier
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Parker's Restaurant at Omni Parker House in Boston claims JFK proposed to Jacqueline Bouvier at Table 40 in 1953.
A conflicting claim exists from Martin's Tavern in Washington, D.C., also marking a proposal site with a plaque.
Omni Parker House, opened in 1855, is the oldest continuously operating hotel in the U.S., with strong JFK ties.
